Mondelez Exec Says Consumer Will Have To Get Used To Chocolates Becoming More Expensive
data.description:
Mondelez International Inc :

data.title: Gold Climbs to New Record — Market Talk
data.description:
1458 ET - Gold futures rise 1.7% to a record $2,931.60 a troy ounce, closing in on $3,000/oz. "Both the fundamental and technical near-term outlooks continue to strengthen the bullish sentiment," Peter Cardillo of Spartan Capital says. Demand for gold from global central banks has also been a factor supporting gold, as has demand from gold ETFs. SPDR Gold shares are up 1.8% in afternoon trading. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1324 ET - WillScot Holdings' initiated cash dividend has two sides to consider. On the one hand, the dividend of 7 cents a share should be well-received and help open up its investor base, says Jefferies' Philip Ng in a research note, though he would prefer excess cash be diverted to M&A. While the dividend may soothe things over after choppy results and stock performance over the last two years, Ng says it may also raise questions that inorganic opportunities are more narrow after it and fellow portable-building company McGrath Rentcorp in September called off a roughly $3 billion merger. Shares are up 1% to $38.40. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1300 ET - Fast-food owner Yum Brands said it plans to shift some of its corporate operations out of Louisville, Ky., the longtime home of its KFC brand. The company says that 100 U.S. corporate KFC workers will be asked to report to Plano, Texas, home to Yum's Pizza Hut division. The chain is asking 90 other U.S. workers now serving in remote roles to begin reporting to the campus where their work occurs. Yum will maintain two U.S. headquarters in Plano and Irvine, Calif, while it will have a smaller footprint in Louisville. (heather.haddon@wsj.com; @heatherhaddon)

1157 ET - General Mills is investing in pet food as it is seeing consumers spend more on their furry companions, CEO Jeff Harmening said during a conference. "We believe the humanization of pets will continue leading pet parents to treat their pets more like members of the family," which will likely drive more spending in the premium pet food category, Harmening said. Pet food is a $52 billion business for General Mills in the U.S. and $130 billion globally. The company acquired White Bridge in December to expand its offerings of wet food for cats, which is the fastest growing pet food category in the U.S. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1134 ET - Southwest Airlines' decision to shed about 1,750 jobs is hard to square away like the others it's issued since its September investor day, says Melius Research's Conor Cunningham and Patrick Coleman in a research note. The analysts say the culture instilled at the carrier is a big factor in its successful history. "The news yesterday, to terminate 15% of corporate positions, goes against much of that culture the company has cultivated over its 53-year history," the analysts say. Other changes at Southwest included eliminating its open seating policy, which came in the wake of activist Elliott Investment Management's stake in the company. "A delicate balance is very much needed here, as culture is difficult to cultivate but easy to ruin," say the analysts. (denny.jacob@wsj.com; @pennedbyden)

1112 ET - H&E Equipment's fleet size and age is a good way for an acquirer to bring down its fleet costs. Steven Ramsey of Thompson Research Group tells WSJ that H&E has a sizable fleet spread across a large branch network, with a fleet age that is much younger than the industry and younger than that of United Rentals and Herc Holdings. "The capex needed to replace the fleet of H&E can be much lower in the next few years, resulting in tremendous free cash flow to the acquirer," Ramsey says. The free cash flow could then be used for debt repayment, allowing the acquirer to pay a higher price. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1106 ET - Oppenheimer thinks that this may not be the end of the line for United Rental's pursuit of H&E Equipment Services. H&E received an offer from Herc Holdings for nearly $105 per share, Schneeberger says, or about $5.3 billion. This tops United Rentals' recent bid of about $4.8 billion. "H&E appears to be an attractive addition for both United Rental and Herc. We view it likely United Rental continues its pursuit of H&E," he says. H&E Equipment surges 15% and Herc Holdings is off 10%. United Rentals is down 3.7%. (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

1059 ET - Brazil faces slow growth and high inflation, UBS BB economists write. The central bank is hiking as inflation drifts away from its 3% target. UBS BB expects the policy rate to reach 15.25% this year (it is at 13.25%) and GDP growth to slow to 1.3% from more than 3% estimated for 2024. Government spending has been blamed for inflation, but UBS BB says outlays could rise further to counter economic slowdown. That would keep interest rates higher for longer. UBS BB says that "a combination of weak growth drivers, deteriorating economic indicators and persistent inflation suggests Brazil is heading toward stagflation in 2025-26." (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1045 ET - Intel's reported consideration of a split-up could be overly time-consuming and complicated, Bank of America analysts led by Vivek Arya say. If the semiconductor maker sells itself to Broadcom, the companies would likely face a lengthy regulatory approval process, Arya says. Intel would beef up Broadcom's central processing capability, and has a strong brand name, something that has previously drawn Broadcom to buy other companies like LSI and Brocade. But Broadcom has about $58 billion in net debt, which puts the company at risk of over-investing in the ecosystem, Arya says. Intel shares are up 10%, Broadcom down 2.7%. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1041 ET - Reports that Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company is thinking about buying Intel suggest a set of regulatory and operational obstacles, Bank of America analysts led by Brad Lin say. The semiconductor companies have different tool sets, programs and cultures that could cause clashes, not to mention the geopolitical complexity of a Chinese company buying a U.S. stalwart as tensions between the two countries are already elevated under the Trump administration, Lin says. There would likely be a long regulatory process from officials in both countries, as well as potential anti-trust concerns if the two companies merged, Lin says. Intel shares are up 10%, TSMC slips 0.8%.(katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1040 ET - Most U.S. metros have become more affordable for buyers and renters over the past year, but renting remains cheaper than buying in all but Detroit and Pittsburgh, according to Realtor.com. Last year, buying was less expensive in six markets, reflecting falling rents and high mortgage rates. The trend leans towards renting, with only Pittsburgh--$229,700 median price--and Detroit--$239,950--favoring buying. Despite a drop in rents from 2023 and 2024, they're still 16.1% higher than in January 2020, impacting renters' budgets. This affordability shift could boost renter households and lower homeownership rates in 2025. (chris.wack@wsj.com)

1036 ET - Intel's consideration of a split-up could be overly time-consuming and complicated, Bank of America analysts say. WSJ reported that Broadcom and TSMC were eyeing separate deals that would break the U.S. chip maker in two. If Broadcom bought a part of Intel, the companies would likely face a lengthy regulatory approval process, the analysts say. Intel would beef up Broadcom's central processing capability, and has a strong brand name, something that has previously drawn Broadcom to buy other companies like LSI and Brocade. But Broadcom has about $58 billion in net debt, which puts the company at risk of over-investing in the ecosystem, the analysts say. Intel climbs 10%, Broadcom is off 2%.(katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

data.title: CONOCOPHILLIPS SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
ConocoPhillips, a leading global energy company specializing in the exploration and production of oil and natural gas, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales and other operating revenues: $54,745 million, decreased primarily due to lower realized natural gas and crude prices.
  • Net Income: $9,245 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year due to lower commodity prices and higher expenses.
  • Earnings per share: $1.90 for the fourth quarter of 2024, indicating a decrease in profitability compared to the previous year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Lower 48 segment contributed 63% of consolidated liquids production and 74% of consolidated natural gas production, highlighting its significance in the company's operations.
  • Geographical Performance:
    • In Alaska, production decreased slightly due to normal field decline, but was offset by new wells online.
    • The Lower 48 saw increased production due to new wells in the Delaware Basin, Eagle Ford, Midland Basin, and Bakken, as well as assets acquired from Marathon Oil.
    • Canada reported increased production due to higher working interest in Surmont and new wells in the Montney, despite planned turnaround activities.
    • The Europe, Middle East, and North Africa segment saw increased production from new wells in Norway and assets acquired from Marathon Oil, despite lower commodity prices.
    • In Asia Pacific, production decreased slightly due to normal field decline, with some offset from development activity in Bohai Bay, China.
  • Sales Units: Total company production in 2024 was 1,987 MBOED, a 9% increase from 2023, driven by acquisitions and new wells.
  • New Production Launches: First production was achieved at Nuna in Alaska, Bohai Phase 5 in China, and Eldfisk North in Norway, marking significant operational milestones.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to increase production to 2.34 to 2.38 MMBOED in 2025, with a focus on maintaining a resilient asset portfolio and meeting global energy demand. ConocoPhillips aims to continue optimizing its portfolio with a focus on low cost of supply and low GHG intensity, aligning with its Climate Risk Strategy.

Strategic Initiatives

  • ConocoPhillips completed the acquisition of Marathon Oil for approximately $16.5 billion, enhancing its portfolio with high-quality, low-cost supply assets and additional LNG capacity.
  • The company focused on optimizing its portfolio by entering into agreements to sell noncore assets within its Lower 48 segments, expected to close in the first half of 2025 for approximately $600 million.
  • ConocoPhillips exercised preferential rights to acquire additional working interests in Alaska's Kuparuk River and Prudhoe Bay Units.
  • In 2024, ConocoPhillips returned $9.1 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases, including $5.5 billion in share repurchases and $3.6 billion in dividends. The company increased its ordinary dividend by 34% to 78 cents per share.
  • ConocoPhillips completed strategic debt transactions, issuing $5.2 billion in new long-term debt and repurchasing $4.1 billion of existing debt, simplifying its capital structure and extending the weighted average maturity of its debt portfolio.
  • For 2025, ConocoPhillips plans to return $10 billion to shareholders through its capital return framework, contingent on current commodity prices. The company announced a first-quarter ordinary dividend of 78 cents per share and plans to invest approximately $12.9 billion in capital expenditures.
  • ConocoPhillips aims to maintain its 'A'-rating and continue its disciplined investment strategy, focusing on low-cost supply and high-return assets. The company also plans to meet its emissions-reduction targets and achieve zero routine flaring by the end of 2025 for its heritage assets.

Challenges and Risks

  • The company faces significant risks related to volatile commodity prices, which can fluctuate widely due to factors beyond its control, such as geopolitical tensions, economic slowdowns, and global health crises. Prolonged periods of low commodity prices could materially affect revenues, operating income, and cash flows, potentially impacting dividend payments and share repurchase programs.
  • The exploration and production of oil and gas is highly competitive, with the company facing competition from alternative fuels and challenges in securing necessary resources and personnel.
  • The company's ability to reduce operational GHG emissions intensity is subject to uncertainties, including government policies, market acceptance of carbon capture technologies, and the development of emissions measurement technologies. The execution of the Climate Risk Strategy could be costly and may require purchasing emission credits, which may not be perceived as acceptable by investors or regulators.
  • The company is also exposed to risks from regulatory changes, such as price controls, production limitations, and export restrictions, which could adversely impact operations. Additionally, the availability of necessary facilities and equipment for production could be affected by market conditions, extreme weather, and regulatory matters.
  • The acquisition of Marathon Oil presents integration challenges, with potential difficulties in realizing expected synergies and benefits. The integration process could result in higher than expected costs and disruptions to ongoing business operations.
  • The company also faces risks related to its capital return program, with potential impacts on share repurchases and dividend payments due to market conditions and financial performance.
  • The company is subject to cybersecurity threats, which could disrupt operations and result in financial and reputational damage. The increasing sophistication of cyber threats, including those enhanced by generative AI, poses a significant risk to the company's data and operational technology systems.
  • The company anticipates continued capital expenditures and operating costs due to compliance with environmental laws and regulations, which are expected to increase in complexity and number. New climate change-related regulations could impact business plans and result in significant expenditures, potentially reducing demand for the company's products.
  • The company is also exposed to political and economic risks in international markets, with potential impacts from changes in foreign governmental policies, geopolitical tensions, and currency fluctuations. These factors could affect the company's ability to operate and maintain licenses in foreign jurisdictions.

SEC Filing: CONOCOPHILLIPS [ COP ] - 10-K - Feb. 18, 2025

data.title: Tennis-Djokovic stunned by Berrettini in Qatar Open first round
data.description:
Novak Djokovic was stunned 7-6(4) 6-2 by Matteo Berrettini in the Qatar Open first round on Tuesday as the Italian enjoyed his first win over the former world number one.

Berrettini played superbly to knock out the twice champion in one hour 35 minutes to secure his first victory against a top 10 opponent since January 2023.

"It's something that I've been looking to do," said former world number six Berrettini, who had lost all four of his previous matches against the Serbian.

"I played against him in the most important tournaments on tour, had the honour and the pleasure to play against him. I wish I could have won one of those matches as well."

World number 35 Berrettini, voted the ATP's comeback player last year, faces Dutchman Tallon Griekspoor in the next round.

The 24-times Grand Slam winner Djokovic, aiming for a 100th ATP title, was playing his first singles match since retiring in the Australian Open semi-finals due to a hamstring injury.

The 37-year-old, who last week said he was "almost fully recovered", had not lost a first-round match since his 2022 Monte Carlo Masters defeat by Alejandro Davidovich Fokina.

Djokovic will now turn his attention to the doubles event, pairing up with Spaniard Fernando Verdasco against Finn Harri Heliovaara and Britain's Henry Pattem in the quarter-finals.

data.title: Cheesecake Factory Likely to Report 'Solid' Q4, UBS Says
data.description:
Cheesecake Factory is likely to report "solid" Q4 same-store sales when it report its earnings, UBS said in a note emailed Tuesday.

UBS said Q4 same-store sales would roughly be in line with expectations. The company's investors are expecting same-store sales of around 1.5%, while analysts have pegged it at 1.6%, according to UBS.

The company's Q4 margins are also likely to be in line with consensus and UBS said it expects Cheesecake Factory to reiterate most of its 2025 guidance.

The investment bank said it expects Q4 revenue of $915.6 million, slightly above the consensus of $912.5 million and also higher than the company's guidance of $905 million to $915 million.

"We expect contribution from marketing, improving guest satisfaction scores, loyalty, and a higher income customer base, with mix headwinds likely to have continued to abate," UBS wrote.

Still, the firm said it was "somewhat cautious" on 2025 given the risk to same-store sales "from a still challenging macro and competitive factors."

The firm has a sell rating on the stock with a price target of $41.

data.title: Raw sugar prices hit two-month high, arabica coffee falls
data.description:
Raw sugar futures on ICE rose to a two-month high on Tuesday as the prospect of substantial exports from India faded, while arabica coffee fell further from a recent record peak.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar settled up 0.1 cent, or 0.5%, at 20.52 cents per lb after setting a two-month high of 20.72 cents.

* Dealers said diminishing sugar production prospects in India had helped to drive a run-up in prices.

* "Lower sugar production means that less sugar is likely to be available for export in India. According to traders, it is questionable whether the quantity of one million tons of sugar approved for export will even be reached," Commerzbank said in a note on Tuesday.

* "The rally in the Indian internal (sugar) market has made it difficult for those exports to be booked and it look like they will fall short," said the McDougall Global View Sugar Report.

* White sugar fell 1% to $541.80 a metric ton.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee settled down 2.15 cents, or 0.5%, at $4.0525 per lb, slipping further from a record high of $4.2995 set a week ago.

* Dealers said the run-up in prices had temporarily lost momentum and the market may fall further in the short-term.

* The market remained underpinned, however, by the tight supplies and the prospect of a smaller arabica crop in top grower Brazil this year.

* Consultancy Safras & Mercado said Brazil farmers sold 88% of the 2024/25 crop so far, ahead of 79% at this time last year and also higher than the five-year average of 82%.

* Robusta coffee rose 0.7% at $5,721 a ton.

COCOA

* New York cocoa futures ​settled up $166, or 1.6%, to $10,491 a ton.

* Dealers said a slowdown in the pace of cocoa arrivals in ports in Ivory Coast had helped to support prices while there were also quality concerns.

* "Funds are leaving cocoa. Since the beginning of 2025, funds in NY have reduced their net long position by 13,086 contracts to 27,304 contracts, while funds in London have reduced their net long positions by 7,478 contracts to 24,458 contracts. This has caused market open interest to dwindle," said broker StoneX in a note.

* London cocoa was little changed at 8,170 pounds per ton.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Tuesday at 3 PM ET
data.description:
S&P 500, Nasdaq Composite Rise

U.S. stocks saw a muted start to the trading week. Intel shares jumped after The Wall Street Journal reported that Broadcom and TSMC were eyeing separate deals that would break the U.S. chip maker in two.

U.S. and Russia Forge New Path on Ukraine After First High-Level Talks in Years

The announcement of a formal process to try to halt the Ukraine war followed talks in which Washington attempted to reset its tense relationship with Moscow.

Fed's Waller Says Policy Uncertainty Shouldn't Dissuade More Rate Cuts

Federal Reserve governor Christopher Waller said the central bank should cut interest rates further if inflation makes more progress back toward target.

Canada Inflation Accelerated Slightly to 1.9% in January

Market expectations for a pause in interest-rate cuts increase after the higher consumer-price report.

German Economic Hopes Rise as Election Nears

Investor confidence rose this month on hopes that a change in government at elections on Feb. 23 could spur a reversal of economic fortunes.

U.K. Wages Gather Pace, Complicating Bank of England's Task Ahead

Wages grew at a faster rate in the U.K. at the end of last year, posing a potential headache for the Bank of England after it cut interest rates again and warned of anemic economic growth this year.

RBA Joins Wave of Global Central Bank Easing

The Reserve Bank of Australia cut interest rates for the first time since 2020 on Tuesday, joining its international counterparts in the global easing cycle.

Trade Splits Will Weigh on World Economy, BOE's Bailey Says

A loss of cohesion in global trade will drag the world's economic growth downward, while the implications for inflation are less easy to call, Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey warned.

An Investing Riddle: Stocks Are in Turmoil but Stock Markets Aren't

While the S&P 500 has been mostly unruffled by DeepSeek and the tariff war, "dispersion" within the index keeps increasing.

Investors Haven't Been This Pessimistic About Stocks Since 2023

Tumultuous policy changes, a potential trade war and sky-high stock prices have turned some investors bearish in 2025.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Tuesday at 3 PM ET
data.description:
Broadcom's Deal Magic Would Get Its Biggest Test With Intel

Buying Intel would show just how far Broadcom's superpower in managing chip acquisitions reaches.

Fidelity Investments Reports Record Annual Profit, Revenue

The stock market's rally drew more money into the firm's funds and spurred trading activity by its customers.

Continental to Cut Around 3,000 Jobs in Automotive Segment

The car-parts company said the cuts amounted to around 10% of its global research-and-development jobs.

Johnson & Johnson Goes to Trial on Third Talc Bankruptcy Plan

Healthcare conglomerate Johnson & Johnson kicks off its bankruptcy plan confirmation Tuesday, in its third attempt to resolve one of the largest mass tort lawsuits through an affiliate it placed into chapter 11 bankruptcy.

Diamondback Energy to Acquire Double Eagle

The oil and natural gas company reached an agreement with Double Eagle in exchange for 6.9 million shares of its common stock and $3 billion in cash.

Heineken CEO Aims to Tap Into Thirst for Zero-Alcohol Beer

The Dutch brewer is seeking to tap into growing demand for low- and no-alcohol beverages, a category that expanded rapidly in recent years as more consumers try to moderate their alcohol intake.

Southwest Airlines to Slash Corporate Workforce in First Mass Layoff

The carrier is trying to cut costs after a battle with an activist investor last year.

'Gut Pop' Is Injecting New Fizz Into the Beverage Aisle

Coke and PepsiCo are jumping into the fast-growing market for prebiotic sodas, now cornered by upstarts Olipop and Poppi.

Scania to Buy Northvolt's Heavy Industry Battery Business

Scania agreed to buy Northvolt's heavy industry business as the battery maker continues to work through a restructuring process.

Herc Holdings Set to Buy H&E Equipment Services

Herc Holdings said it will acquire rental business H&E Equipment Services in a cash and stock deal, offering a 14% higher price than United Rentals' proposal.

data.title: New Zealand house prices softer but sales activity improves, REINZ says
data.description:
New Zealand house prices fell in January as more houses came onto the market, the Real Estate Institute of New Zealand said on Wednesday.

Seasonally adjusted median house prices in January were down 1.0% on December figures, but up 1.1% from a year earlier, REINZ data showed.

The number of houses sold nationwide on a seasonally adjusted basis rose 4.8% from December and 90.0% from January 2024 when the market was in the doldrums.

REINZ Chief Executive Jen Baird said January was traditionally a slower month because of the holiday season.

"First home buyers and owner-occupiers are still the largest groups at open homes, with salespeople reporting investor interest in pockets of the country," Baird said in a statement.

New Zealand house prices are still about 16% below their peak after the central bank rapidly increased the official cash rate. While the central bank has started reducing rates , the general weakness in the economy means economists are not expecting a sharp rise in property prices.

data.title: CANADA FX DEBT - Canadian dollar weakens, benchmark yield climbs
data.description:
The Canadian dollar weakened against the greenback on Tuesday, and the yield on benchmark government debt climbed.

The loonie was trading 0.1% lower at C$1.4187 to the greenback, or 70.49 U.S. cents, after trading in a range of 1.418 to 1.4212.

Canadian government 10-year bond yields (CA10YT=RR) rose 8.8 basis points to 3.194%. The yield on similar U.S. government benchmark debt rose to 4.5444%.

U.S. March crude futures rose $1.11 to settle at $71.85 a barrel on Tuesday.

Canada's annual inflation rate accelerated to 1.9% in January, up from 1.8% in December, Statistics Canada said. Analysts polled by Reuters had expected an annual rate of 1.9%

data.title: Nike Stock Rises With Kim Kardashian's Skims Partnership — Update
data.description:
By Sabela Ojea

Nike became the best performer on the Dow Jones Industrial Average Index after the sportswear company said it will collaborate with Kim Kardashian's Skims to launch a new brand.

The stock on Tuesday rose 4.7% to $76.46 in afternoon trading. Despite the intraday gains, shares have dropped 26% over the past 12 months.

NikeSkims in spring will launch at select retail locations in the U.S. and on Nike's website. A global expansion will follow in 2026, the companies said.

The partnership marks the first time Nike is working with another company to launch a new brand. The strategy comes amid a turnaround for the Beaverton, Ore.-based company, led by Chief Executive Elliott Hill, as it aims to recapture some of the market share it lost to more innovative competitors.

"Nike has been accused of missing out on cultural relevance, so it is interesting to see them partner with another brand to create a sense of cultural relevance," said analyst Simeon Siegel at BMO Capital Markets. "Nike shouldn't need to buy their cultural validation, though."

Nike seems to be thinking differently about its strategy as it goes after companies dominating the lucrative athleisure market, such as Lululemon, Alo Yoga and Fabletics, said Neil Saunders, analyst of GlobalData.

"Skims is a hot brand with a lot of traction, so Nike is hoping to use some of Skims' heat to create desirability around new assortment," Saunders said.

Still, he doesn't think NikeSkims will be an automatic overnight success. The companies would have to work on promoting the brand outside the U.S., Skims' biggest market.

"When they launch the brand there will be a lot of interest and buying activity, but there's a lot more work to be done to make it very solid and a very visible part of the market," Saunders said.

Write to Sabela Ojea at sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix

data.title: Permian Basin Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >PBT
data.description:
Permian Basin Royalty Trust (PBT) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on February 18, 2025.

On February 18, 2025, the Registrant issued a press release announcing its monthly cash distribution to unitholders of record on February 28, 2025. The press release is attached hereto as Exhibit 99.1 and is incorporated herein by reference.

This Report on Form 8-K is being furnished pursuant to Item 2.02, Results of Operations and Financial Condition. The information furnished is not deemed "filed" for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, is not subject to the liabilities of that section and is not deemed incorporated by reference in any filing under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/319654/000095017025022075/2025-02_permian_basin_8-.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/319654/000095017025022075/0000950170-25-022075-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Banco Santander announces over $2 bln investment in Mexico in three years
data.description:
Spanish bank Santander will do various investments amounting to over $2 billion in Mexico during the next three years, the company's Executive Chair Ana Botin said on Tuesday.

Botin made the announcement during an event in Mexico's capital where the company presented it's 100% digital bank, Openbank, that is already operating in Mexico.

Earlier on Tuesday, Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum announced the amount of the investment without giving further details, after meeting with Botin on Monday.

"We are announcing an investment program over the next three years of more than $2 billion, 42 billion pesos," Botin said during the event, detailing that the amount will be designated to Openbank, Santander Bank itself and other items.

"Mexico is the country where we see the greatest growth potential," she said, adding that it is one of the countries where the company is "going to continue investing the most from now on".

Santander's announcement marks an increase in the group's investments in the Latin American Country.

In 2003, Botin met with former Mexican President Andres Manuel Lopez Obrador and posted the bank's commitment to invest around 500 million dollars a year in the country until 2025.

Santander's Mexican subsidiary is one of the country's largest banks.

data.title: U.S. Soccer and Westwood One Reach English-Language Audio Rights Agreement
data.description:
Westwood One Sports to Bring U.S. Soccer to Audio Listeners Nationwide;

First Broadcast Set for 10th Annual SheBelieves Cup

CHICAGO, Feb. 18, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — U.S. Soccer and Cumulus Media’s Westwood One have reached an agreement for Westwood One Sports to serve as the new domestic English-language audio partner of U.S. Soccer. The multi-year exclusive partnership gives Westwood One Sports broadcast rights to all U.S. Soccer Federation-hosted matches, including all USWNT and USMNT friendlies, Concacaf Nations League Quarterfinal matches, and the SheBelieves Cup. The first broadcast will be Thursday night’s SheBelieves Cup game featuring the USWNT vs. Colombia at 8 pm ET.

With more than 9,800 affiliated radio stations, Westwood One is the largest audio network in the United States. Westwood One’s expansive reach will include Westwood One’s audio distribution network and the Cumulus Podcast Network and affiliated platforms. Westwood One Sports will be the home of U.S. Soccer podcasts on radio and other audio platforms in the U.S. through 2030.

“We want U.S. Soccer fans to have as many avenues as possible to engage with our matches, players, and coaches, and this partnership allows them to find us on Westwood One Sports’ vast network,” said David Wright, Chief Commercial Officer for U.S. Soccer. “Westwood One will bring our matches to life in exciting ways while meeting our fans wherever they are listening.”

"The addition of U.S. Soccer to our Westwood One Sports platform is an exciting milestone for us. Bringing both the men's and women's national team programming to our listeners enhances our commitment to delivering world-class sports coverage," said Collin Jones, President of Westwood One and EVP, Corporate Strategy and Development, Cumulus Media. "This partnership not only provides fans with unparalleled access to the action on the pitch but also creates valuable opportunities for our advertisers to connect with a passionate and engaged audience. We look forward to amplifying the excitement of U.S. Soccer across our network for many years to come."

As part of the partnership, U.S. Soccer will be launching a new podcast series, the first of many that will bring exclusive insights and behind-the-scenes access to its senior national teams, youth national teams and extended national teams. The podcast will feature interviews from across the American soccer landscape, will focus on how U.S. Soccer is growing the game and will highlight the unprecedented lineup of upcoming events including the SheBelieves Cup, the U.S. Open Cup and the 2026 FIFA Men’s World Cup.

Westwood One Sports joins Warner Bros. Discovery, NBCUniversal Telemundo and Fútbol de Primera, as U.S. Soccer’s domestic media rightsholders.

About Westwood One Sports

Westwood One Sports is home to some of the most exciting sports broadcasts on radio. In addition to being the exclusive network radio partner to the NFL since 1987 – featuring regular and post-season NFL football, including the playoffs and the Super Bowl – its other extensive properties include NCAA Basketball, including the NCAA Men’s and Women’s Tournaments and the Final Four®; The Masters; NCAA Football; U.S. Soccer Federation, both Men’s and Women’s National Teams, and other marquee sports events. Westwood One also distributes and represents Infinity Sports Network. On social media, join the Westwood One Sports community on Facebook at facebook.com/westwoodonesports, on Instagram at instagram.com/westwoodonesports, and Twitter at twitter.com/westwood1sports. For more information, visit www.westwoodonesports.com.

About Cumulus Media 

Cumulus Media is an audio-first media company delivering premium content to over a quarter billion people every month — wherever and whenever they want it. Cumulus Media engages listeners with high-quality local programming through 400 owned-and-operated radio stations across 84 markets; delivers nationally-syndicated sports, news, talk, and entertainment programming from iconic brands including the NFL, the NCAA, the Masters, CNN, the AP, the Academy of Country Music Awards, and many other world-class partners across more than 9,800 affiliated stations through Westwood One, the largest audio network in America; and inspires listeners through the Cumulus Podcast Network, its rapidly growing network of original podcasts that are smart, entertaining and thought-provoking. Cumulus Media provides advertisers with personal connections, local impact and national reach through broadcast and on-demand digital, mobile, social, and voice-activated platforms, as well as integrated digital marketing services, powerful influencers, full-service audio solutions, industry-leading research and insights, and live event experiences. Cumulus Media is the only audio media company to provide marketers with local and national advertising performance guarantees. For more information visit www.cumulusmedia.com.

About U.S. Soccer 

Founded in 1913, U.S. Soccer has been the official governing body of the sport in the United States for more than 100 years. As U.S. Soccer looks toward the future amid an unprecedented moment of opportunity, it has aligned its efforts around five strategic pillars: Grow the game by increasing youth and adult participation and accessibility to the sport; Foster best playing environments through quality of referees and coaches, and commitment to participant safety; Develop winning teams through solidified pathways and success of professional leagues; Grow the soccer economy to fuel reinvestment by increasing membership, fandom and commercial success; and Create a world-class organization through revitalized structure and culture, best-in-class talent and more. For more information, visit ussoccer.com/ourvision. 

Cumulus Media/Westwood One Contact: Lisa Dollinger, Dollinger Strategic Communication, lisa@dollcomm.com and 512.633.4084

data.title: Street Color: Capital One Financial, Discover Financial Services Shareholders Approve Capital One's Proposed Takeover of Discover
San Juan Basin Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >SJT
data.description:
San Juan Basin Royalty Trust (SJT) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on February 18, 2025.

On February 18, 2025, the San Juan Basin Royalty Trust (the "Trust") issued a press release, a copy of which is attached hereto as Exhibit 99.1, announcing that it would not declare a monthly cash distribution to the holders of its units of beneficial interest ("Units") for February due to continued lower natural gas prices and cumulative excess production costs that continued to exceed proceeds during the production month of December 2024.

In accordance with general instruction B.2 to Form 8-K, the information in this Form 8-K shall be deemed "furnished" and not "filed" with the Securities and Exchange Commission for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended, or otherwise subject to the liabilities of that section.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/319655/000095017025022084/sjt_february_2025_8-k_no.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/319655/000095017025022084/0000950170-25-022084-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Fletcher Building Ltd. 1H Rev NZD3.583B
Constellation forgoes earnings call for first time since spinoff
data.description:
Constellation Energy , the largest U.S. nuclear power operator, skipped its quarterly earnings investor call on Tuesday for the first time since going public three years ago.

Constellation did not immediately respond to a request for comment. The lack of a call surprised some analysts.

Maryland-based Constellation held a management call last month, when it announced a proposed $16.4 billion acquisition of private natural gas and geothermal provider Calpine in one of the biggest U.S. power industry deals.

A discussion on Tuesday would have allowed for questions about Constellation's full-year 2024 results, the Calpine acquisition and other company matters, Bank of America analysts said in a note. The cancellation surprised some analysts.

Constellation, which was spun off from Exelon in early 2022, released stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter earnings earlier on Tuesday. Its shares rose about 2.5% at mid-afternoon.

Constellation is among the biggest stock market winners in the past year, rising roughly 150% from a surge in demand from artificial intelligence data centers.

The independent power producer has recently landed lucrative contracts, including one to restart a Three Mile Island nuclear reactor for Microsoft data centers.

BofA and J.P. Morgan analysts said a meeting between Constellation and regulators on Thursday could be a catalyst for the stock. The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission may address Constellation's complaint over rules governing co-located commercial arrangements, in which data centers directly connect to a power plant.

Co-location has become a possible solution for quickly powering data centers without the wait times for connecting to the broader grid, but critics have raised reliability and cost concerns over the setup.

data.title: Hims & Hers Health Shares Fall After Morgan Stanley Downgrade

Trading volume stood at over 17 million shares against a daily average of roughly 14.6 million.

data.title: US Stocks Little Changed as Investors Eye Fed and Tariffs
data.description:
The S&P 500 remained little changed in afternoon trading, hovering near record highs, while the Dow Jones and Nasdaq each declined more than 0.3%.

Investors assessed the possibility of an end to the Ukraine war following the first top-level negotiations between the US and Russia since the 2022 invasion.

Weakness in consumer discretionary and communication services, including a 3.7% drop in Meta Platforms and a 2% decline in Amazon, pressured the broader market.

In contrast, energy stocks outperformed, with Halliburton rising 2.9% and Valero Energy gaining 2.6%.

Meanwhile, market participants closely monitored policy developments from the Fed and the White House, particularly regarding tariffs and interest rates.

Treasury yields climbed, with the benchmark 10-year yield reaching 4.54%, as traders sought clarity on the Fed’s rate trajectory.

Earnings season also remained in focus, with upcoming reports from Baidu and Alibaba adding to uncertainty over the global economic outlook.

data.title: FTX begins initial fund distributions to customers as its bankruptcy recovery forges on
data.description:
An initial batch of customers of bankrupt exchange FTX are beginning to receive their funds over the next few days, with others set to get their share in the coming months.

Convenience class customers, those claiming up to $50,000 will begin seeing their funds in the next one to three business days, according to a statement released Tuesday by FTX. The next round of distributions will take place on April 11, FTX said.

"We are pleased to commence initial distributions today and set the timeline for our next distribution," said John J. Ray lll, the plan administrator of the FTX recovery trust and FTX Debtors CEO. "FTX appreciates our customers and creditors' patience and collaboration throughout this complicated process. Our work is not over – we intend to continue our recovery efforts and returning funds to additional claim classes."

Funds will be available through BitGo and Kraken, FTX said.

FTX's bankruptcy plan was approved by a Delaware judge in October 2024, two years after the exchange filed for bankruptcy. Under the plan, 98% of creditors will receive at least 118% of their claim value in cash. The plan garnered criticism from some, including Sunil Kavuri, a representative of the largest FTX creditor group. Kavuri said the estate should pay out cryptocurrencies in kind rather than the dollar value when the exchange filed for bankruptcy back in 2022.

FTX's former CEO, Sam Bankman-Fried, was found guilty in November 2023 of seven criminal counts, including two counts each of wire fraud and conspiracy to commit wire fraud, and was sentenced to nearly 25 years in prison. Sister trading firm Alameda also subsequently fell, and its CEO Caroline Ellison was sentenced to two years for her role in the downfall of FTX.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2024 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: Polaris Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Polaris Inc., a leading manufacturer in the powersports industry, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, detailing the company's financial and operational performance over the past year. The report highlights significant declines in key financial metrics, operational challenges, and strategic initiatives aimed at bolstering future growth and market competitiveness.

Financial Highlights

Sales: $7,175.4 million, a decrease of 20% from 2023 primarily due to decreased shipments in all segments and lower net pricing driven by higher promotional costs.

Gross Profit: $1,466.8 million, a decrease of 25% from 2023, primarily due to lower net pricing driven by higher promotional costs, product mix, and decreased leverage of fixed costs.

Operating Income: $290.6 million, a decrease of 59% from 2023, impacted by decreased shipments and lower net pricing.

Net Income from Continuing Operations: $111.2 million, a decrease of 78% from 2023, primarily due to decreased shipments and lower net pricing.

Diluted Net Income from Continuing Operations Per Share: $1.95, a decrease of 78% from 2023, reflecting lower net income.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Polaris operates in three main segments: Off Road, On Road, and Marine. The Off Road segment, which includes ORVs and snowmobiles, accounted for 79% of total sales in 2024. The On Road segment, which includes motorcycles and moto-roadsters, contributed 14% to total sales, while the Marine segment, which focuses on boats, made up 7% of total sales.

Geographical Performance: Sales in the United States, which represent the largest market, decreased by 21% in 2024 due to lower shipments across all segments. Canadian sales also saw a decline of 24%, primarily due to decreased snowmobile shipments. Sales in other countries decreased by 10%, with lower ORV and motorcycle shipments being the main contributors.

Sales Units: In North America, ORV retail sales remained flat at 805,000 units in 2024. However, snowmobile unit retail sales for the 2024-2025 season-to-date period through December 31, 2024, were down by low-forties percent. The Marine segment saw a significant decrease in unit sales, with U.S. pontoon unit retail sales down mid-teens percent and deck boat unit retail sales down mid-thirties percent.

New Product Launches: In 2024, Polaris introduced several new products, including a redesigned RZR Pro lineup and new trim offerings, as well as refreshed RANGER models. The Marine segment launched the 2025 model year boats for Bennington, Godfrey, and Hurricane brands, featuring new models and redesigned features.

Future Outlook: Polaris continues to focus on innovation and new product development, with an emphasis on electrification initiatives to position the company as a leader in powersports electrification. The company also plans to maintain its market share leadership in the ORV segment and expand its presence in international markets.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Polaris Inc. has focused on strategic initiatives to enhance its financial flexibility and operational efficiency. The company has engaged in corporate restructuring programs to drive operating efficiencies, incurring costs of $23.4 million related to such activities in 2024. Additionally, Polaris has made strategic investments in developed technology assets, amounting to $62.7 million in 2024, to bolster its product offerings and market competitiveness.

Capital Management: In 2024, Polaris Inc. repurchased 1.0 million shares of its common stock for $82.7 million, which positively impacted diluted net income per share. The company declared and paid cash dividends of $2.64 per share, continuing its long-standing practice of returning value to shareholders. Polaris also managed its debt portfolio by issuing $500 million in 6.95% Senior Notes due in 2029 and maintaining compliance with all debt covenants. The company has a robust credit facility with $1.1 billion of availability as of December 31, 2024, and has amended its credit agreements to optimize financial ratios and leverage.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Polaris Inc. plans to continue its focus on maintaining strong liquidity and capital management practices. The company aims to leverage its existing cash balances, operating cash flows, and credit facilities to fund operations, product development, and shareholder returns. Polaris is committed to sustaining its dividend growth, as evidenced by the recent increase in its quarterly cash dividend, and will explore further opportunities for share repurchases under the authorized buyback program. The company also intends to pursue strategic investments that align with its long-term growth objectives.

Challenges and Risks

Market and Operational Risks: Polaris Inc. operates in highly competitive markets, including off-road vehicles, motorcycles, and marine segments. The company faces intense competition based on price, quality, reliability, styling, and product features. Additionally, the company is exposed to risks related to its global operations, including political and economic instability, trade tensions, and regulatory changes.

Macroeconomic Risks: Polaris faces several macroeconomic risks, including sensitivity to economic conditions that impact customer spending, particularly in North America and Europe. Fluctuations in economic factors such as employment levels, inflation, and consumer credit availability could reduce demand for its products. The company is also vulnerable to supply chain disruptions and increases in raw material costs, which could negatively impact profitability.

Regulatory and Cybersecurity Risks: Regulatory risks include compliance with extensive safety, environmental, and trade regulations, which could result in significant expenses or operational changes. Additionally, cybersecurity threats pose a risk to the company's information systems and data integrity.

Management Strategies: In 2024, Polaris experienced a 20% decrease in sales, primarily due to decreased shipments across all segments and higher promotional costs. The company reported a significant decline in net income and gross profit, attributed to lower net pricing and reduced sales volumes. Management highlights the need to address these challenges by optimizing production schedules and enhancing operational efficiencies. The company also emphasizes the importance of maintaining strong dealer and distributor relationships to support sales growth.

Market Risk Management: Polaris is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, which can impact the pricing of its products in international markets and the costs of imported components. The company actively manages this risk through foreign exchange hedging contracts. Additionally, the company faces risks related to interest rate fluctuations, which could affect its debt servicing costs and financial flexibility.

SEC Filing: Polaris Inc. [ PII ] - 10-K - Feb. 18, 2025
